meta.method: 6 specialist finder agents + 1 independent adversarial verifier per target (24 agents total)

target.name: fillet-chamfer-success
target.metric: count of failed fillet/chamfer features (lower is better) on a fixed seeded corpus of feasible cases (radius/length ≤ 20% of the smallest adjacent dimension, so every case SHOULD succeed)
target.measure_cmd: cd /home/thomash/Koding/projects/SindriCAD/sidecar && .venv/bin/python tools/eval_fillet_corpus.py --corpus tools/corpus_fillet.json  # for each doc: part, errors, bodies = builder.rebuild(doc, diagnostics=diag); count errors whose feature_id is the fillet/chamfer feature; print 'failed=N/500 rate=...' plus the edgeOpFailed diagnostic breakdown (per-edge vs combination failures, builder.py:1055-1063)
target.baseline_capture: Generate corpus once with fixed seed (random box/cylinder primitives + join/cut booleans, then fillet or chamfer edge sets selected by axis/all/ofFace at conservative sizes), check corpus_fillet.json in, run the eval. Generation reuses the exact document shapes test_smoke.py already builds (test_smoke.py:61-75 is a one-case version of this).
target.goal: cut baseline failure count by 50%, then iterate toward <2% failure rate on the feasible corpus
target.why_loopable: Each failure class is an independent fix in _handle_fillet/_handle_chamfer (builder.py:1066-1089), which today make ONE monolithic OCCT call and give up: per-edge fallback (do the edges that pass, report the rest), splitting edge sets into tangent chains, ordering edges before the OCCT call, retrying with slightly reduced radius on near-max cases, pre-unifying the body. The existing _report_edge_failures probe (builder.py:1033-1063) already distinguishes 'per-edge' from 'combination' failures, giving the loop a built-in failure taxonomy to chew through class by class.
target.gaming_risks: Agent could shrink radii or regenerate an easier corpus — pin the checked-in corpus by hash. Agent could 'succeed' by silently skipping failing edges without shrinking the metric honestly — pair the failure count with a secondary assert in the harness that the resulting body volume changed vs. pre-fillet (a fillet that did nothing counts as failed). Corpus feasibility rule (radius ≤ 20% of adjacent dim) is data in the corpus file, not code the loop edits.
target.evidence: builder.py:1066-1076 (_handle_fillet): single fillet(edges, radius=r) call, any exception fails the whole feature; same for chamfer at 1079-1089. builder.py:1532-1541: a failed feature is recorded as {feature_id, message} in errors and the build continues — the metric is directly harvestable from rebuild()'s return. builder.py:1033-1063: _report_edge_failures already probes which individual edges fail, emitting an 'edgeOpFailed' diagnostic — the eval gets a per-class breakdown for free. Project memory confirms fillet/chamfer failures are a live UX issue (commit f08d315 'paint failed fillet/chamfer edges red').

(3) Baseline signal CONFIRMED by a live 60-case seeded probe (box+box join/cut, radius/length ≤ 20% of smallest primitive dim): failed=29/60 (48%), fully deterministic, with a usable taxonomy (12 chamfer 'try smaller length' — 8 combination / 4 per-edge; 16 fillet failures; 1 selector miss 'no edge found'). Noise is effectively zero: OCCT is deterministic on identical input and the corpus is seed-pinned. (4) Meaningful, not vanity: fillet robustness is the canonical parametric-CAD pain point and project history (commit f08d315, painting failed fillet edges red) confirms it is a live UX issue. WEAKNESSES that survive but don't kill: (a) tools/eval_fillet_corpus.py and corpus_fillet.json do not exist — harness_effort 'hours' is honest but real, and sidecar/tools/ is currently UNTRACKED in git (gen_manybodies.py uncommitted), so the corpus/harness must be committed before hashing means anything; (b) the '≤20% of smallest adjacent dimension' feasibility rule does NOT guarantee OCCT feasibility — booleans create thin walls and seam-adjacent edges where the true local clearance is smaller than any primitive dimension, so an unknown fraction of my 48% baseline is genuinely infeasible and the '<2%' end goal is speculative; keep '50% reduction' as the goal and recalibrate the absolute target after the first failure-taxonomy pass; (c) the proposal's own fix idea 'retry with slightly reduced radius' is silent geometry corruption in a parametric CAD app (user asked r=2.0, gets r=1.8 with no error) and its proposed volume-changed check does NOT catch it, nor does it catch a per-edge fallback that fillets 1 of 12 edges and reports success — both change volume; (d) 'no edge found' selector misses are a different defect class (geom_select) and must be counted separately or excluded by construction. GAMING VECTORS CONFIRMED REAL: the loop edits builder.py which contains both the op AND the error-recording path, so exception-swallowing, partial-success-as-success, and radius-shrinking all inflate the metric past the proposed volume check — the refined invariants below close them.
verdict.refined_metric: failed-count on a frozen 500-case corpus where a fillet/chamfer feature counts FAILED if ANY of: (a) its feature_id appears in rebuild() errors; (b) harness-side face-count invariant fails: len(faces) of the resulting body < len(faces) of the pre-op body + n_edges, where n_edges (expected resolved edge count) is computed at corpus-generation time and stored per case — this catches partial per-edge fallbacks AND selector weakening; (c) for the box-only subset (~half the corpus), volume delta vs the pre-op body deviates >2% from the analytic expectation stored in the corpus (convex 90-degree fillet removes (1-pi/4)*r^2*L per edge; chamfer removes d^2/2*L) — this catches silent radius reduction. The harness computes faces/volume itself via build123d on the returned bodies, never trusting builder-emitted diagnostics for pass/fail. Corpus generation rule tightened: only edges whose TRUE local clearance (including boolean-created walls, checked at generation by measuring distance to nearest non-adjacent face) is >= 3x the radius. Selector misses ('no edge found') reported as a separate counter, not in the headline metric. Goal: 50% reduction from frozen baseline; recalibrate the absolute (<2%) target only after the first taxonomy pass identifies genuinely-infeasible cases.
anti_gaming: Commit sidecar/tools/ first (it is currently untracked), then pin corpus_fillet.json AND eval_fillet_corpus.py by sha256 inside the loop's acceptance step; the harness refuses to run if either hash mismatches, and both files are in the loop agent's read-only set

Holdout corpus: generate a second corpus with a different seed, never shown to the loop agent; acceptance requires comparable improvement on the holdout to catch corpus special-casing (e.g. hardcoded radius/shape patterns in builder.py)

Independent post-invariants computed by the harness itself from the returned body shapes via build123d (face-count delta >= stored n_edges; analytic volume delta within 2% on the box subset) — success is errors-empty AND invariants-pass, so exception swallowing, partial per-edge fallback counted as success, and silent radius reduction all still register as failures

Store expected resolved edge count per case in the corpus at generation time, so weakening resolve_edges in geom_select.py to return fewer/easier edges trips the face-count invariant instead of shrinking the metric

Explicitly ban 'apply a smaller radius than requested without recording a feature error' in the loop instructions — reduced-radius retry is only acceptable as a user-facing suggestion, never as silent success

Cross-check regression gate: the existing test_smoke.py suite (including test_error_naming, which REQUIRES an infeasible r=100 fillet to still fail and be named) must stay green every iteration, preventing the degenerate fix of making fillet never raise

Invariants beyond counts: total merged-part volume within 0.01% and per-body _owners map sizes equal to baseline — face/body counts alone let an agent gut the provenance owner-map maintenance (a named hotspot) undetected
Holdout: also measure 5-cleanb.sindri each accepted iteration and fail on >5% regression; optionally add an unannounced gen_manybodies.py-generated doc to catch corpus overfitting
Correctness gate: sidecar test suite must pass in the same iteration as any accepted perf win; fresh subprocess per timing run (no module-level warm state can leak across runs)

target.name: selector-survival
target.metric: % of edge/face selector references that resolve to the INTENDED entity after an upstream parametric mutation (higher is better), over a fixed seeded corpus of (document, mutation) pairs
target.measure_cmd: cd /home/thomash/Koding/projects/SindriCAD/sidecar && .venv/bin/python tools/eval_selector_survival.py --corpus tools/corpus_selectors.json  # prints e.g. 'survive=163/200 rate=0.815' — harness generates docs (box/cylinder/boolean stacks + fillet/chamfer referencing edges via the SHIPPING legacy nearest/axis selectors and via v2 match), applies one upstream edit (dimension change, added hole, moved sketch), calls builder.rebuild(doc), and checks the resolved entity's midpoint/centroid against a parametric oracle computed from the doc parameters (never from the resolver)
target.baseline_capture: Write the harness + generate corpus once with a fixed seed, check corpus_selectors.json into the repo, run eval once with current geom_select.py. All pieces exist today: test_selector_v2.py:33-48 already shows how to author fingerprints from real edges (edge_fp/face_fp), and rebuild() is directly importable (test_smoke.py:11).
target.goal: ≥95% survival on the legacy-selector half of the corpus and ≥99% on the v2-match half, from an expected baseline well below that (legacy nearest is documented to collide)
target.why_loopable: Many independent small wins, each testable in isolation: geom_select.py:57-66 exposes 7 tunable tolerances and 6 scoring weights; _resolve_one (geom_select.py:248-269) has tie-band and nth logic; legacy 'nearest' can be upgraded per-callsite; new fingerprint fields (adjacent-face count, dihedral) can be added one at a time; each failing corpus case is a distinct micro-bug. This is exactly the topological-naming problem MCAD systems grind down incrementally.

WEAKNESSES that survive: (a) the oracle is authored by the same loop agent being scored — largest gaming surface; (b) 95% on the legacy-nearest half may be information-theoretically unreachable (stateless sidecar gets only a stale point; mirrored twins under symmetric mutations are genuinely ambiguous), and an unreachable goal is a gaming incentive — legacy target must be set per-category after baseline; (c) one aggregate rate conflates two halves with different goals. verdict.refined_metric: Two separate rates over a frozen, sha256-pinned corpus of >=200 (document, mutation, selector, oracle) tuples: legacy_rate (shipping axis/normal/nearest selectors) and v2_rate (match fingerprints), printed as 'legacy=NNN/NNN rate=0.XXX v2=NNN/NNN rate=0.XXX' plus a per-category breakdown (concentric, mirrored-twin, boolean-stack, dimension-change, added-feature, moved-sketch). A case survives iff resolve_edges/resolve_faces on the rebuilt mutated doc returns an entity whose midpoint/centroid is within the corpus-pinned tolerance of the analytically precomputed oracle point, AND the eval independently confirms that oracle point identifies exactly one entity of the expected curve/surface type in the rebuilt shape (else the case is 'invalid', counted against neither side, and reported). Loop success = v2_rate >= 0.99 AND legacy_rate >= (baseline-informed target, provisionally 0.90) AND no per-category regression AND test_selector_v2.py green in the same invocation. Generator quotas hard cases: >=20% of corpus must be concentric or mirrored-twin.
anti_gaming: sha256-pin BOTH corpus_selectors.json AND eval_selector_survival.py; the pinned hashes live in the loop wrapper/CI (outside sidecar/tools/), and the run refuses with nonzero exit if either hash mismatches — this closes 'regenerate corpus', 'loosen tolerance in eval code', and 'edit the comparator' in one move

oracle midpoints and tolerances are precomputed analytically at corpus-generation time and frozen INTO the corpus JSON; the eval never derives expected values from geom_select.py, and additionally asserts the oracle point uniquely identifies exactly one entity of the expected type in the rebuilt shape (a wrong or ambiguous oracle marks the case invalid, not passed)

holdout set: generate a second corpus with a different seed, store it OUTSIDE the repo (or encrypted/gitignored), never expose it to the loop agent; run it only at human review checkpoints — a frozen-corpus gain that doesn't transfer to the holdout is overfitting to 200 cases and rejects the iteration

per-category rates with a no-regression rule: an aggregate increase that drops any category (e.g., tuning W_POS up until concentric passes but mirrored regresses) fails the iteration even if the headline number rises
invariant cross-check in the same eval invocation: run test_selector_v2.py and test_smoke.py; any failure zeroes the score, so the resolver cannot be special-cased into a corpus-only shape

quota hard cases in the checked-in generator (>=20% concentric/mirrored-twin, mutations that actually move the referenced geometry) so a future 'regenerate corpus at new seed' request cannot silently produce an easier distribution

set the legacy-half goal AFTER baseline capture, per-category, and explicitly mark information-theoretically ambiguous cases (mirrored twin + symmetric mutation with stale point) as a separate 'known-ambiguous' bucket excluded from the legacy target — an unreachable goal is itself a gaming incentive

verdict.refined_metric: Per-CASE pass rate on a frozen boolean corpus. A case = one JSON .sindri document (template-instantiated: coincident-face joins, tangent/near-tangent cylinder joins at offsets {0, 1e-3, 1e-5}, thin-wall cuts at 0.1–0.5mm, multi-body bridging extrudes, facet-imported BREP bodies boolean'd against native solids — 200–500 cases across ~10 templates x seeded parameter jitter). A case PASSES iff, after rebuild(doc): (a) the errors list is empty; (b) len(bodies) equals the recorded expected body count; (c) for every body, BRepCheck_Analyzer(shape.wrapped).IsValid() is True; (d) per-body solid count equals the recorded expectation; (e) per-body total volume is within the recorded band (closed-form where the template permits, else seeded 200k-point Monte Carlo membership on the analytic CSG expression, band = max(0.3%, MC 3-sigma)). Expected values are computed at generation time from the CSG definition, NEVER from rebuild output. Script prints one line: 'cases=487/500 rate=0.974 ocp=<version> corpus_sha=<sha256>'. Metric = rate; goal >=0.99. Runtime budget: under 5 minutes single-process (measured 0.06s for an 8-feature doc, so 500 heavier docs fits).

NOISE: negligible (verified deterministic across cache modes on this machine; single-machine eval, fixed venv). ASSUMED (not verified): determinism across OCCT/build123d upgrades — an upgrade will legitimately shift tessellated volumes and requires a human re-baseline; that is the one recurring maintenance cost.

Mitigate: classifier also requires message distinctness (count unique messages per unique corruption; a single catch-all string collapsing hundreds of cases scores as raw) and requires the message to name the feature/field it complains about. Second risk: suppressing errors entirely (empty errors list) — count a corrupted doc that produces NO error and no geometry change as a silent failure, worse than raw.
target.evidence: builder.py:1542-1554 — non-ValueError exceptions become `f"{label} failed ({type(ex).__name__})"`, explicitly documented as 'meaningless to a user'. server.py:828-829 — the WS handler's blanket `except Exception as ex: await ws.send(_err(req_id, str(ex)))` puts raw Python text on the wire; e.g. a rebuild request missing 'document'/'ops' or an export missing req["document"] (server.py:795) yields the bare KeyError repr `"'document'"` as the user-facing message. Contrast with the ~25 curated ValueError sites (builder.py:998, 1070, 1098, 1134, 1164, 1221, 1274...) showing the target quality bar already exists in-repo.

verdict.reasons: VERIFIED: (a) two-tier convention is real — builder.py:1532-1541 surfaces hand-authored ValueErrors verbatim (39 raise-ValueError sites, more than the ~25 claimed), builder.py:1542-1556 collapses everything else to f"{label} failed ({type(ex).__name__})" with an in-code comment calling it 'meaningless to a user'; (b) server.py:827-828 blanket `except Exception: _err(req_id, str(ex))` puts raw Python reprs on the wire, and req["document"] at ~795 yields bare KeyError text, exactly as claimed; (c) 13 .sindri seeds exist in src-tauri/ (4-170 features each, ~1355 total); (d) 26 feature handlers (builder.py:1344-1371), so per-handler incremental wins are real; (e) rebuild() at builder.py:1374 exposes resume/snapshots_out, enabling prefix-cached mutation runs. REFUTED/NOT-YET-TRUE: measure_cmd is NOT runnable today — sidecar/tools/ contains only gen_manybodies.py; fuzz_docs.py and the --mode msgquality enumerator must be built first (cost shared with the sibling doc-fuzz target, but baseline is NOT capturable today). WEAKNESSES FOUND: naive one-mutation-per-field enumeration over all 13 seeds is ~16k full OCCT rebuilds (hours-to-days per run) — must dedupe by (feature_type, key_path, mutation_kind) to ~400 mutations hosted in smallest seeds; the proposed regex misses OCCT names not ending in 'Error' (StdFail_NotDone); the proposed global-distinctness anti-gaming rule would falsely penalize legitimate repeated messages like 'no edge found to fillet'; the rate's denominator ('failure responses') is shrinkable by swallowing errors. All fixable — see refined_metric. Value: genuine hardening for a repo about to go public with external doc producers, though not daily-driver UX since the frontend writes valid docs. Noise: near-zero once mutations run with per-mutation timeouts and per-seed subprocess isolation (an OCCT segfault otherwise kills the harness and silently truncates the corpus).
verdict.refined_metric: raw-or-silent error rate = (raw + silent + misattributed) / N over a FROZEN mutation manifest, where N = the fixed set of mutations that produced an error at baseline (recorded in a committed expectations file). Corpus: dedupe mutations by (feature_type, top-level key path, mutation kind ∈ {delete, type-swap}) — one representative per class hosted in the smallest seed containing that feature type (~400 mutations, not 16k), executed via rebuild()'s resume/snapshot API, each seed in a subprocess with a per-mutation timeout (timeout/crash of the harness process counts as raw). Classification is POSITIVE, not regex-blacklist: a message is GOOD only if all three hold — (1) errors list contains exactly the corrupted feature_id and the rest of the timeline still built (geometry of other features unchanged vs baseline); (2) the message contains the corrupted key name OR the feature label/type; (3) the message does not contain a Python/OCCT exception class name (\b\w+(Error|Exception|Failure|NotDone)\b) or repr fragments ('object has no attribute', 'NoneType', bare-quoted key). silent = baseline expected an error but the mutated rebuild returns no error AND geometry hash equals the uncorrupted baseline hash. Goal: <5% raw-or-silent from measured baseline (expected >30%).
anti_gaming: Freeze the corpus: commit a manifest of (seed sha256, mutation list sha256); fuzz_docs.py --mode msgquality refuses to run and exits nonzero if hashes mismatch, so the loop agent cannot prune hard mutations

Expectations file recorded once at baseline (per-mutation: errored? geometry hash of unmutated build) and committed; silent failures counted against this frozen set so shrinking the denominator by swallowing errors raises, not lowers, the score

Positive classifier (message must name the corrupted field or feature label) instead of a raw-pattern blacklist — rewording the generic fallback to 'something went wrong' still scores raw because it names neither; note the cheapest passing fix (a KeyError wrapper emitting "{label}: missing '{key}'") is a genuine UX improvement, which is the desired failure mode of gaming
Holdout: 3 of the 13 seeds excluded from the per-message report the loop agent sees; final score computed over all seeds, so special-casing visible corpus messages shows up as a train/holdout gap

Invariant cross-check per mutation: rebuild must still return geometry and every non-corrupted feature's output must match baseline (guards against 'fixing' messages by rejecting the whole document up front, which would break the MCAD failed-feature-is-a-no-op semantics at builder.py:1533-1539)
Harness integrity: fuzz_docs.py classifier lives outside the improvement loop's writable scope, or its sha256 is pinned in the loop config and checked before scoring
Per-seed subprocess isolation with timeouts, and any harness crash/truncation scores the remaining mutations as raw — prevents inflating the score by making hard mutations segfault the runner

(2) Both claimed escape classes reproduce live with a one-liner: rebuild({'features':['not-a-dict']}) raises AttributeError ('str' object has no attribute 'get') from the pre-try access at builder.py:1512+1524 (f = features[i]; prov = f.get('type') before the try at :1526), and rebuild({'parameters':3,...,'bodyVisibility':7}) raises AttributeError ('int' object has no attribute 'items') at builder.py:1406. server.py:284-292 _apply_doc_ops and the blanket except at server.py:828-829 shipping str(ex) raw also verified. Metric is deterministic (seeded RNG, count-based) — noise is near zero. REFUTED/WEAKENED, three real problems: (A) RUNTIME KILLS THE SPEC AS WRITTEN — I timed every seed: full rebuild takes 7-30s each (5-cleanf.sindri: 29.7s, 170 features; only ddr_crash is fast at 0.31s). Since rebuild deliberately continues past failed features (MCAD no-op semantics, builder.py:1536-1555), a single-point mutation still pays a near-full rebuild. 1000 mutants ≈ 3-6 CPU-hours per measurement — useless as a loop inner metric. Must fuzz truncated seeds (first ~10 features) or ~200-400 mutants. (B) SURFACE SMALLER THAN CLAIMED — builder.py:1542 already has a broad per-feature `except Exception`, so every mutation landing INSIDE a feature dict is already contained; 'dozens of independent wins' is wrong. The real escape surface is ~5-10 sites (pre-try feature access, doc-level fields at :1401/:1406/:1482, non-list features, post-loop normalization). Loop saturates in a handful of iterations. (C) SCOPE MISMATCH — the measure_cmd (rebuild(doc) only) never exercises the cited server.py:_apply_doc_ops sites; that evidence is unreachable by this fuzzer unless an ops-fuzz mode is added (worth adding: an exception mid-_apply_doc_ops leaves _DOC_STATE half-mutated — a genuine delta-corruption bug class). (D) The proposed anti-gaming ('classify messages containing exception reprs') is weak: a top-level `except Exception: return error` in rebuild() zeroes the metric, breaks no pristine seed, and produces no repr — it passes all stated mitigations. Needs the message-dominance and attribution invariants below. Still worth keeping: escapes are real, confirmed today, baseline capturable in hours, and fixes protect users loading hand-edited/version-skewed .sindri files plus the stateful incremental-cache worker (rebuild_cached, builder.py:1951+) from partial-state corruption. Value is real but bounded — short loop lifetime and the server's existing top-level catch already prevents actual app crashes (only garbage error strings reach the UI), hence 6/10 not 8.
verdict.refined_metric: escapes-per-corpus (lower is better): fuzz_docs.py generates, with seeded RNG, 400 mutants from a FROZEN truncated corpus (each shipped .sindri truncated to its first 10 features, plus ddr_crash.sindri.sindri whole; corpus JSON written once and SHA256-pinned) using mutation classes {delete-key, type-swap-value, truncate-list, null-feature, non-dict-feature, non-dict-doc-field, non-list-features}. Mutants run as rebuild(doc) in batched subprocesses with a 120s per-batch timeout. Escape = exception escaping rebuild(), nonzero subprocess exit (segfault), or batch timeout. Add a second mode --ops that fuzzes server._apply_doc_ops payloads the same way (covers server.py:284-292 and verifies _DOC_STATE is either fully applied or fully rejected). anti_gaming: SHA256-pin sidecar/tools/fuzz_docs.py and the truncated-seed corpus file in the harness config; the scorer recomputes hashes before every run and refuses to score if the loop agent edited either.

Message-dominance invariant: if any single error-message string accounts for >50% of contained mutants, the run FAILS — a blanket top-level try/except yields one repeated generic message, while per-site guards yield varied, field-specific messages. Attribution invariant: >=60% of contained mutants must return an error carrying a feature_id or named field path (a top-level catch cannot know which feature/field was bad).

Golden regression cross-check: after each loop iteration, rebuild all 13 pristine seeds and require errors == [] plus body counts equal to a frozen golden record captured at baseline — prevents 'validation' that rejects valid documents to dodge escapes.
Holdout: acceptance runs 3 RNG seeds never used during the loop, plus one mutation class (e.g. deep nested type-swap) withheld from the loop-visible mutator and only enabled at acceptance.
Segfault/timeout channel is observed by the harness (exit code, wall clock), never self-reported by the code under test.

(b) BUT the metric as specced is NOT emittable today: the rebuild reply carries NO volume anywhere — body keys are exactly [edges, etag, faceCount, faceIds, faceOwners, id, indices, name, positions] (observed on the wire via probe); test_fullstack.py:96-111 never compares volume either. The harness must compute volume itself (divergence theorem over the returned mesh) or the product must be modified to emit it. (c) The 'evidence' test is broken against the current working tree: test_fullstack.py:40 connects with no token, but server.py:712-726 (_authorized, uncommitted changes) closes it 1008 unauthorized — I reproduced this on a free port; it also fails outright on the default port because 8765 is held by the user's live app (server.py:45, SINDRI_SIDECAR_PORT env exists as the fix). The server also interleaves {'status':'building'} progress frames with the same request id, which the test_fullstack-style recv loop mistakes for the reply — my probe hit this live. (d) The 'golden corpus' is fiction: all 13 .sindri files are GITIGNORED (.gitignore:27 'src-tauri/*.sindri', git ls-files count = 0), are debug leftovers of ONE model family (every doc starts with the same 'import ddr' feature embedding a ~1.4MB brep; 76-151 features each; one is literally ddr_crash.sindri.sindri), so '13 docs x 3 formats' is really 1 shape x 3 with 12 near-duplicates, unfrozen and silently editable by a loop agent. (e) The 100%-at-0.5%/0.1% goal is UNREACHABLE BY DESIGN for curved shapes via mesh formats: measured live, a radius-8 cylinder round-tripped via STL comes back with bbox 8.079 (≈1.0% off, 10x the 0.1% gate) and 30 faceted faces; via 3MF 8.065 (0.8% off). Mesh import deliberately refacets for editability (_refacet_clean builder.py:751+, MAX_IMPORT_FACES=2000 at builder.py:673, explicit 'SindriCAD edits prismatic CAD models' rejection at builder.py:920-925). The worst gaming risk is therefore the metric incentivizing PRODUCT DAMAGE: the shortest path to green is weakening _refacet_clean / raising MAX_IMPORT_FACES / relaxing _canonicalize's own 0.5% volume guard (builder.py:874-875), trading away the editability the import path exists for. (f) STEP round-trip, by contrast, measured clean: cylinder came back as 3 analytic faces with exact bbox — those cells are meaningful and loopable. (g) Noise: OCCT is deterministic; flake sources are operational (port collision with the live app, progress frames, 90s import budget on the heavy ddr docs) — all fixable in the harness, so signal is fine once the grid is sane. ASSUMED (not verified): rebuild wall-time for the 150-feature ddr docs; they built in the app historically but per-cell cost of full stateless rebuilds of 1.4MB-import docs likely makes the proposed grid minutes-per-run. Net: the target survives only because the STEP half is genuinely valuable interop coverage (currently 1 verified cell) and every defect has a concrete fix; the proposal's grid, goal, and evidence as written are wrong. harness_effort 'hours' is optimistic — a committed corpus must be authored from scratch, call it a day-plus.
verdict.refined_metric: Split the grid by what each format can promise. Corpus: author and COMMIT sidecar/testdata/roundtrip/*.json — >=10 small parametric feature-docs covering box, cylinder, revolve, loft, shell, fillet/chamfer, boolean cut, multi-body (reuse test_fullstack.py doc shapes; do NOT use the gitignored src-tauri/*.sindri ddr files), plus a SHA-256 manifest. Cells: (A) STEP-roundtrip: export -> import -> rebuild the imported brep; pass iff harness-computed volume (divergence theorem over returned mesh triangles at fixed tolerance=0.1) within 0.5% AND harness-computed bbox from positions within 0.1% AND imported face count <= original face count + 2 (analytic recovery check). (B) STL/3MF export-fidelity (no re-import): parse the exported file's triangles directly; pass iff watertight (every edge shared by exactly 2 triangles, consistent winding) AND divergence-theorem volume within 0.5% of the source brep's mesh volume at the same tolerance. (C) Optional prismatic-only STL re-import sub-grid (box/boolean docs only) where refacet is expected exact at 0.5%/0.5%. Output: 'ROUNDTRIP k/N maxerr=x% corpus=<manifest-sha> script=<self-sha>'; exit nonzero if any cell is skipped, any manifest hash mismatches, or N < full grid. Goal: 100% of A + B cells; C reported but gated separately.
anti_gaming: Commit the corpus with a SHA-256 manifest; the script verifies every doc hash and exits nonzero on mismatch or on N < full grid, so docs can't be trimmed, edited, or allowlisted silently (the current 'corpus' is gitignored and thus fully forgeable).

Freeze tolerances (0.5% vol, 0.1% bbox, tessellation tol=0.1) as constants in tools/roundtrip_fidelity.py and have the script print a self-hash in its output line; the loop config treats any diff to the eval script or manifest as human-review-only.

Compute volume and bbox HARNESS-SIDE via divergence theorem over the returned mesh positions/indices — never trust a server-reported number the loop agent could add and inflate; cross-check bbox against the server's bbox field as a consistency invariant.

Editability guard (blocks the highest-value cheat): the script asserts builder.py's MAX_IMPORT_FACES == 2000 and includes one negative cell — a dense curved STL that MUST still be rejected with the 'didn't reduce to a clean editable model' error; if the agent weakens _refacet_clean or the face cap to pass mesh cells, this cell fails.
STEP cells require imported face count <= original + 2, so an agent can't pass volume/bbox by shipping faceted brep blobs through the STEP path.
Holdout: keep 2-3 corpus docs out of the committed grid (separate manifest, run manually/weekly) to detect corpus-specific special-casing in exporters.py or import_geometry.

Operational determinism: the script must spawn its own server on an ephemeral SINDRI_SIDECAR_PORT with the minted TOKEN (server.py:729-734) and skip progress frames ({'status':'building'}) — otherwise runs against the user's live 8765 instance or stale binaries produce phantom deltas.
